seoTitle: Dangar Island vs Dangarsleigh — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Dangar Island and Dangarsleigh. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Dangar Island vs Dangarsleigh: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Dangar Island and Dangarsleigh.
heading: Dangar Island vs Dangarsleigh — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Dangar Island, New South Wales and Dangarsleigh, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Dangarsleigh has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Dangar Island (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Dangar Island or Dangarsleigh safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Dangar Island has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Dangarsleigh scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Dangar Island scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Dangarsleigh scores 15/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Dangar Island or Dangarsleigh?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Dangarsleigh has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
